hidden_state.scenario_summary: Synthetic mixed-evidence case where invoice confusion is the clearest customer-facing symptom, but limited operational noise makes the commercial answer still require review.
latent_facts: Billing surprise is the most concrete customer-facing problem in the packet.
Operational evidence is too weak to support an SLA remedy.
The case should stay conservative and route to finance review.
likely_failure_modes: Model overcommits to an outage or SLA claim because an incident note exists.
Model ignores the billing-led nature of the case and routes away from finance review.

case_packet.title: Synthetic ambiguous mixed-evidence case 10

notes: Customer has raised both operational and billing concerns in the same thread.

pricing_notes: Invoice preview includes 18 burst GPU-hours above commitment.

usage_record.telemetry_summary: A brief degradation notice overlapped with customer billing confusion, but usage remained mostly stable.
anomalies: Invoice preview and customer expectation remain materially misaligned despite no CRM mismatch.

incident_record.customer_impact_summary: Customer noticed minor stalls, but the billing confusion is the dominant concern.
incident_record.engineering_summary: Engineering saw signs of service degradation, but incident evidence is not yet sufficient to confirm a single cause.

case_packet.customer_note: We saw degraded performance, but now billing also looks off. Can you help us understand what is actually going on?
case_packet.policy_snippet: Do not apply a contractual remedy until incident classification and billing variance are both confirmed.
case_packet.calculator_output: Invoice preview reflects 18 burst GPU-hours, but the final commercial explanation still needs finance review.
case_packet.visible_case_summary: Synthetic mixed-evidence case where billing surprise is stronger than the operational signal.

adjudication_notes: Ambiguous cases should preserve uncertainty and avoid overclaiming a remedy.
This case follows the billing_dominant ambiguity profile and should route according to the strongest unresolved signal.
ground_truth.reference_customer_note: We found a mismatch between the invoice preview and the commercial explanation available in the current records, so we are routing this for finance review before sending a final answer. At this stage, we are not treating it as an SLA credit case.
ground_truth.reference_internal_note: Owner: shared_revops_finance. Action: hold_for_finance_review because the root cause remains unknown, customer impact is invoice_confusion, and the strongest unresolved signal is commercial rather than contractual outage exposure.
